lever_summary: Dense F64/I64 cumulative scan. eval_cumulative materialized the input to a Vec<Literal> (to_vec) and scanned each line per-element with as_f64()/as_i64() reads + reduce_real_literal/Literal::I64 writes. New eval_cumulative_dense scans a clone of the contiguous as_f64_slice()/as_i64_slice() backing per line and returns a dense output, bypassing all Literal materialization and per-element dispatch.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA / typed columnar storage applied to prefix scan
alien_graveyard.mapping: scan contiguous native f64/i64 in place instead of materializing + reading/writing a 24-byte tagged-union per element; the cumulative analog of the dense reductions

method: Same-worker comparison on ts2: dense (vector_f64) input vs a Vec<Literal>-backed reference run in the same process.
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- cumsum_64k_f64 --save-baseline cum

change.lever: Added eval_cumulative_dense(tensor, axis, reverse, int_init, float_init, int_op, float_op); eval_cumulative calls it after parsing axis/reverse, returning None (generic path) for non-dense / non-F64/I64. Per-line base offset and scan order match the generic loop exactly.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: same per-line base offset, same ascending (or reversed) scan order along the axis; each element read once before its overwrite
isomorphism.op: unchanged: same per-line float_init/int_init seed and float_op/int_op (Cumsum +, Cumprod *, Cummax jax_max_f64/i64::max, Cummin jax_min_f64/i64::min) applied in the same order
isomorphism.output: F64: reduce_real_output_dtype(F64)==F64 so output literal is from_f64(acc), matching new_f64_values; I64: Literal::I64(acc) matching new_i64_values
isomorphism.errors: as_f64_slice()/as_i64_slice() are Some only for F64/I64 dense storage; complex, F32/F16/BF16, and Literal-backed inputs return None and run the unchanged generic path; empty axis/total returns None (generic handles the empty early-return); stride overflow is checked identically

validation.name: dense_cumulative_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib --release reduction
validation.result: passed: 34 tests incl dense_cumulative_bit_identical_to_literal_path (Cumsum/Cumprod/Cummax/Cummin x forward/reverse x axis 0/1, multi-line [3,40], f64 by bits + i64)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
validation.result: passed (release dodges shared-target proc-macro rmeta drift in debug)
